items.title: Vrijetrappenspecialist Burger bekent: 'Ik heb vanochtend nog geoefend'
items.summary: Wouter Burger kroonde zich zaterdagmiddag tot matchwinner door een vrije trap heerlijk binnen te krullen tegen Bayer Leverkusen. De middenvelder van 1899 Hoffenheim maakte alweer zijn vierde doeplunt dit seizoen.

items.title: Africa Cup of Nations final buildup, Premier League news, and more – matchday live
items.summary: ⚽ News, buildup and discussion before the day’s action ⚽ Fixtures | Tables | Afcon final preview | Email us here Meanwhile, title-chasers City were left red-faced after losing the Manchester derby to bitter rivals United. Michael Carrick enjoyed a dream start to life as United’s interim head coach and won the fans over in the best possible way. Elsewhere in the Premier League, Arsenal missed the opportunity to go nine points clear at the top of the table with a 0-0 draw away at Nottingham Forest. The Gunners missed several opportunities to take the lead, but the main talking point was a penalty incident that was cleared by VAR following a brief check. Mikel Arteta spoke very strongly about the handball incident at full-time. Continue reading...

items.title: Summerville straalt na treffer voor West Ham in derby: 'Ben nu een échte Hammer'
items.summary: Crysencio Summerville heeft dit seizoen zijn eerste doelpunt voor West Ham United in de Premier League te pakken. Uitgerekend op bezoek bij Tottenham Hotspur was de 24-jarige vleugelspeler trefzeker. Hij straalde na afloop van oor tot oor.

items.title: It’s so toxic at Spurs even West Ham were shocked: Frank’s race looks run | Jacob Steinberg
items.summary: Saturday’s visiting fans know a thing or two about being a dysfunctional club but the level of hate inside Tottenham’s gleaming home surprised even them It comes to something when the level of discontent at Tottenham had people who have spent all season watching West Ham wondering if it was all a bit much. There were cries of mutiny whenever Guglielmo Vicario tried to play out from the back, jeers at half-time and, of course, sustained boos when another home defeat for Thomas Frank’s nervous wreck of a team ended with one last cross from Djed Spence drifting sadly out of play in the ninth minute of added time. At times it felt like not knowing how to leave the room after accidentally walking in on a bitter row between a warring couple. How is anyone supposed to function in such a poisonous atmosphere? Somehow, the malaise at West Ham felt mild by comparison. At least the anger in east London tends to be focused mainly at the board for the failure to kick on since swapping Upton Park for the soullessness of Stratford in 2016. It is not targeted at the team or the manager as much, whereas the grumbling at Spurs seems to fly in all directions, especially as the brutal departure of Daniel Levy five months ago has left fans without one standout candidate to be their hate figure. Continue reading...

items.title: ‘I do remember everything’: Katie McCabe on Arsenal, passion and WCL partying
items.summary: Full-back was at the centre of club’s Champions League celebrations and she knows how to party and recover Katie McCabe had the last laugh in May. After Arsenal’s phenomenal Champions League win over Barcelona, in which she was part of a back four that didn’t concede a foul against the three-time European champions, Arsenal partied hard and McCabe was front and centre of the social media posts from inside the club’s afterparty. At the celebration outside the Emirates Stadium two days later, the full-back was a highlight, shades on, leading the 10,000 crowd in singing her own chant before shushing them and kicking off a rendition of the final goalscorer Stina Blackstenius’s song to the tune of Karma Chameleon, getting a huge cheer when she proclaimed “red is in my bloooood” and being spotted having to run to catch up with the coach before it left the ground when the players finished their third day of celebrations. Continue reading...

items.title: Premier League sporting directors: who are they, and how much power do they all have?
items.summary: A guide to each club’s setups, from Chelsea’s multi-headed structure to Brentford’s smooth planning and Manchester United’s muddles The appointment of Andrea Berta as sporting director in March was greeted with much enthusiasm by Arsenal’s supporters, given his impressive track record in more than a decade at Atlético Madrid,. The Italian, who began his career in finance, has made an instant impression. Known as a shrewd negotiator, the suave and softly spoken 54-year-old masterminded Arsenal’s outlay of more than £250m in the summer that included the arrivals of Viktor Gyökeres, Eberechi Eze and Noni Madueke. Berta takes the lead on recruitment in consultation with Richard Garlick, who was promoted to chief executive in September, the manager, Mikel Arteta, and the co-chair Josh Kroenke. James Ellis, a former scout who then spent two years as head of recruitment, was appointed as technical director in the summer and is tasked with “delivering the club’s long-term player progression strategy”, with a focus on creating a pathway from academy to first team. Ed Aarons Continue reading...

items.title: Wissel brengt spektakelmachine NEC aan het wankelen
items.summary: NEC is 2026 begonnen zoals het vorig jaar eindigde: vol aanvallend voetbal, spektakel en doelpunten. De ploeg van trainer Dick Schreuder won zaterdag op bezoek bij NAC Breda met 3-4. Na een uitstekend eerste half uur zorgde een noodgedwongen wissel ervoor dat de Nijmegenaren toch nog een heet Avondje NAC beleefden.

items.title: Engelse pers ziet bevrijding Man Utd: 'Een vernietigend oordeel over Amorim'
items.summary: De Engelse media zagen dat er een last van de schouders is gevallen bij Manchester United. Onder interim-manager Michael Carrick won Manchester United de derby van Manchester City . 'Rúben Amorim was de manager die nooit echt in de legende van United leek te geloven.'

items.title: Seahawks dominate 49ers to advance to NFC championship game
items.summary: Seattle Seahawks found a new level of dominance as they stormed to a 41-6 win over San Francisco 49ers in what was their first playoff game in front of home fans in almost a decade.

items.title: McIlroy, Lowry chase DP World Tour win in Dubai as Elvira leads LIVE!
Allen emotional after Bills loss: 'I let my team-mates down'
items.summary: Buffalo Bills quarterback Josh Allen was left in tears as he shouldered the blame for Saturday's Divisional Round defeat to the Denver Broncos.

items.title: VI Live: Derby Day in de Eredivisie
items.summary: Zondag is het Derby Day in de Eredivisie. We trappen af met de Derby van het Noorden tussen SC Heerenveen en FC Groningen, vervolgen met de Twentse Derby Heracles Almelo - FC Twente en sluiten af met het treffen tussen Feyenoord en Sparta Rotterdam, dat in een apart liveblog wordt behandeld. Verder is er ook buitenlands voetbal. Volg het hier in de VI Live.

items.title: Australian Open LIVE! Norrie in first-round action with Raducanu to come
Sources: PSG finalizing deal for Barcelona starlet
items.summary: Paris Saint-Germain are finalising the remaining details for the transfer of Dro Fernández, the latest prodigy from Barcelona's academy, sources confirmed to ESPN.

items.title: Arteta: Arsenal denied clear penalty in Forest draw
items.summary: Mikel Arteta has said Arsenal were denied a "very clear penalty" in Saturday's 0-0 draw at Nottingham Forest.

items.title: Wrexham captain McClean exits, joins Derry City
items.summary: Wrexham have announced that their club captain James McLean has moved to Derry City.

items.title: Slot 'understands' Liverpool fans' boos after draw
items.summary: Arne Slot said he "completely understands" the frustration of the Liverpool fans who booed the team following Saturday's 1-1 draw with Burnley in the Premier League.

items.title: Man United deliver huge blow to Man City title hopes with shock derby win
items.summary: Manchester City's title bid is as good as over after Manchester United won the midfield battle at Old Trafford.

items.title: Arsenal stumble in draw against resilient Forest
items.summary: Arsenal missed the chance to extend their lead at the top of the Premier League table to nine points, with a goalless draw against a determined Nottingham Forest.

items.title: Ruthless Man Utd beat Man City in dream start for Carrick
items.summary: Michael Carrick gets off to the perfect start in his first match as interim manager for Manchester United with a two-goal derby win against Manchester City, leaving Pep Guardiola's side winless in their past four matches.
